title: Travel reimbursement policy
content: Travel reimbursement policy at Globex Corp. Employees may be reimbursed for travel expenses incurred while on company business. Eligible expenses include economy-class airfare with Delta Airlines or United Airlines, ground transportation, lodging up to 250 USD per night at approved hotels such as Marriott and Hilton, and meals up to 75 USD per day. Receipts are required for any single expense over 25 USD. Reimbursement requests must be submitted through the Workday portal within 60 days of the trip's return date. CFO Sarah Chen reviews and approves all reimbursement requests over 2000 USD. Engineering and Sales staff traveling to the San Francisco headquarters or the Austin office should book through the Globex travel desk. For international travel to the Dublin EMEA office, employees must obtain pre-approval from their department lead at least 14 days in advance.

title: Equipment purchasing policy
content: Equipment purchasing policy at Globex Corp. Employees may purchase work equipment up to 1500 USD without pre-approval. Purchases over 1500 USD require written approval from the department lead. All equipment must be procured through the company's approved vendor list, which includes Apple, Dell, Lenovo, and Logitech. Common approved devices include the MacBook Pro 16-inch for engineering staff and the Dell XPS or Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon for general office use. Peripheral purchases such as Logitech MX keyboards and Dell UltraSharp monitors do not require department lead approval if under the 1500 USD threshold. Equipment purchases are reimbursed within 14 business days of receipt submission through Workday. Marcus Johnson, VP of IT, oversees the approved vendor program and handles exceptions for non-listed vendors. Asset tagging is performed by the IT team at the Austin office on the first Monday of each month.

title: Paid time off and sick leave policy
content: Paid time off and sick leave policy at Globex Corp. Full-time employees accrue 1.67 days of PTO per month, totaling 20 days per year. Sick leave accrues separately at a rate of 1 day per month up to a maximum of 10 days. PTO requests must be submitted through Workday at least two weeks in advance for absences longer than three days. Sick leave does not require advance notice but must be reported by 9 AM on the day of absence via Slack to the employee's direct manager. HR Director Linda Garcia administers the PTO program and handles all escalations. Unused PTO carries over up to 5 days into the following calendar year; unused sick leave does not carry over. Employees on parental leave or medical leave covered by FMLA continue to accrue PTO during their absence. Bereavement leave of up to 5 days is granted separately and does not count against PTO.

title: Remote work policy
content: Remote work policy at Globex Corp. Globex supports a hybrid work model with offices in San Francisco, Austin, and Dublin. Employees may work remotely up to three days per week with manager approval. Fully remote arrangements require approval from both the department head and People Operations, currently led by Chief People Officer Jennifer Kowalski. Remote employees must maintain core overlap hours of 10 AM to 2 PM in their assigned office's time zone. Staff assigned to the San Francisco HQ default to Pacific Time, Austin staff to Central Time, and Dublin EMEA staff to GMT or IST as appropriate. All remote staff are expected to be active on Slack in the #remote-work channel during core hours. Equipment for remote setups, including standing desks from Uplift and ergonomic chairs from Herman Miller, may be reimbursed up to 1000 USD via the standard equipment purchasing policy.

title: Code of conduct and ethics policy
content: Code of conduct and ethics policy at Globex Corp. All employees, contractors, and interns are expected to act with integrity and respect at all times. The Ethics Committee, chaired by General Counsel David Park, reviews all reported violations and recommends disciplinary action when warranted. Reports may be submitted anonymously through the whistleblower hotline operated by EthicsPoint, or directly to David Park or HR Director Linda Garcia. Conflicts of interest, including outside employment with companies such as Acme Industries, Initech, or any direct competitor of Globex, must be disclosed in writing to the Ethics Committee within 30 days of becoming aware of the conflict. Mandatory ethics training, delivered through the LearningHub platform, is required annually for all employees and quarterly for managers. Retaliation against any employee who reports a violation in good faith is itself a violation and will be investigated by the Ethics Committee.

title: Onboarding program
content: Onboarding program at Globex Corp. New hires participate in a two-week structured onboarding program coordinated by Onboarding Manager Patel Krishnan. Week one covers company orientation, IT setup, and benefits enrollment through Workday. Week two includes department-specific training delivered through the LearningHub platform and shadowing sessions with assigned buddies. Engineering hires are paired with senior engineers from their team, while Sales hires shadow account executives in the Austin or San Francisco office. All new hires are issued a MacBook Pro or Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon, a Logitech MX keyboard, and a Dell UltraSharp monitor on their first day. New hire orientation sessions are held every other Monday at 9 AM Pacific Time. The 30-60-90 day check-ins are conducted by the employee's direct manager and reviewed with HR Director Linda Garcia at the 90-day mark.

title: Performance review process
content: Performance review process at Globex Corp. Globex conducts formal performance reviews twice per year, with calibration cycles concluding in Q1 and Q3. The review process is administered through Lattice and uses a 5-point rating scale. Engineering, Sales, and Marketing each follow their own career ladders with role-specific competency rubrics. CEO Jennifer Kowalski leads the executive calibration session in March and September, alongside CFO Sarah Chen and VP of Engineering Marcus Johnson. Self-reviews, peer feedback, and manager assessments feed into the calibration discussion. Promotions are typically announced within two weeks of the calibration session. Compensation adjustments take effect on April 1 and October 1 respectively. Performance Improvement Plans, when warranted, are administered by HR Director Linda Garcia in coordination with the employee's direct manager and may run for 30, 60, or 90 days depending on the role and severity.

title: Health insurance and benefits
content: Health insurance and benefits at Globex Corp. Full-time employees are eligible for comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage starting on the first day of the month following their hire date. Medical coverage is provided by BlueCross BlueShield with a choice of PPO or HMO plans. Dental coverage is provided by Delta Dental, and vision coverage by VSP. Health Savings Accounts and Flexible Spending Accounts are administered by Fidelity. Globex contributes 80 percent of the employee premium and 50 percent of dependent premiums. Open enrollment runs from November 1 through November 30 each year, with coverage effective January 1 of the following year. Benefits Coordinator Tina Rodriguez handles all enrollment questions and life-event changes. The Employee Assistance Program is operated by ComPsych and offers up to 8 free counseling sessions per year. 401(k) plans are administered through Fidelity with a 4 percent company match on employee contributions up to 6 percent of salary.
